exemplar.title: How does the A24 film âEverything Everywhere All at Onceâ employ the narrative structure of the butterfly effect to explore and resolve themes of generational trauma within Chinese American immigrant families?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the A24 film âEverything Everywhere All at Onceâ employ the narrative structure of the butterfly effect to explore and resolve themes of generational trauma within Chinese American immigrant families?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with clear purpose and focus evident from the title through to the conclusion.
The research question is clearly stated, sharply delimited, and consistently connected to the analysis throughout the essay.
Section signposting and the roadmap in the introduction reinforce a coherent methodological focus.
weaknesses: Minor digressions (e.g. extended plot summary) slightly weaken concision at times.
The conclusion contains some redundant phrasing across consecutive paragraphs.
Methodology relies heavily on nonâscholarly sources and lacks explicit justification for source selection.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands comfortably meet the 5â6 descriptor (topic clarity and research-question focus), while methodology ranks at the top of the 3â4 band.  Overall, the investigation demonstrates purposeful focus and effective question framing but falls just short of a perfect â6â due to repetition and limited methodological justification.

strengths: Terminology such as âmultiverse,â âmise-en-scÃ¨ne,â and âgenerational traumaâ is used mostly accurately and consistently.
The student demonstrates a coherent understanding of film concepts and cultural context, integrating visual analysis with theoretical ideas.
Figures are wellâcaptioned and embedded to reinforce source use and analysis.
weaknesses: Occasional imprecision in psychological terms (e.g. conflating bipolar disorder with general mood swings).
Some secondary sources are paraphrased rather than critically integrated, and one claim lacks a full citation.
The opening definition of trauma relies on broad descriptors rather than peer-reviewed literature.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows more than âgoodâ application of knowledge and terminology, with largely effective use of sources.  A few lapses in academic rigor and precision prevent it from reaching the top of the 5â6 band, but the overall coherence and consistent subjectâspecific vocabulary justify a score of 5.

strengths: The research draws on a purposeful range of twenty-plus sources plus the primary film, with most clearly relevant to the research question.
Analysis of cinematic techniques (color, costume, mise-en-scÃ¨ne) is well linked to thematic arguments about trauma and the butterfly effect.
The argument follows a logical progression and integrates well-chosen scene analyses and dialogue to support conclusions.
weaknesses: Critical evaluation lacks explicit engagement with counter-arguments or potential limitations.
Several analytical paragraphs rely on general commentary before grounding points in concrete evidence.
Some suggestions for deeper counter-analysis (e.g. rebuttals, alternative theoretical frameworks) remain unaddressed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall research quality, analysis depth, and argument development are strong, with well-supported conclusions and consistent relevance to the research question.  Minor gaps in explicit critical evaluation of alternative viewpoints keep the score just below the perfect â12.â  Hence, an 11 accurately reflects excellent but not entirely exhaustive critical thinking.

strengths: The structure follows IB conventions with a clear title page, table of contents, numbered sections, and appropriately integrated figures.
Headings and visual elements enhance navigability and overall readability.
MLA-style citations are consistently used throughout.
weaknesses: Formatting inconsistencies, such as a misspelled directorâs name and incomplete figure captions, interrupt flow.
Some broken URLs and typographical artifacts (e.g. MathML overlines) detract from polish.
Page numbers are referenced in the ToC but not visible, and duplicate conclusion paragraphs suggest final proofreading lapses.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the essay meets core presentation requirements and exhibits a coherent structure, the presence of multiple minor formatting and proofreading errors prevents a top-band score.  The overall presentation is sound but not flawless, warranting a score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: Method is mostly textual/visual analysis of the primary film supplemented by secondary on-line articles, popular interviews and a handful of academic sources.  Citations are supplied and a Works Cited list is present, showing informed selection, but the range is weighted toward journalistic sites (ScreenRant, CBR, Medium) rather than scholarly criticism.  There is no explicit explanation of how sources were selected or any reference to established film-analysis methodologies, so the strand fits comfortably in the upper half of the 3-4 band.

Headings, tables and figures are used effectively, and MLA-style citations are attempted; nonetheless, there are inconsistencies (duplicate conclusion paragraphs, some broken URLs, uneven figure formatting).  Page numbers are mentioned in the ToC but not visible in the excerpt, and a few typographical artefacts (e.g. over-lines) interrupt flow.  These minor lapses correspond to the mid-band (1-2 issues present) rather than perfect execution.

Research draws on roughly twenty named sources plus the primary text, and each source relates in some way to trauma, Asian-American culture, cinematography or symbolism, i.e. mostly relevant to the question.  The scholarly depth is moderate rather than extensive, but the selection is purposeful and largely on target, meriting the mid-band (4-6) with a score of 5.

The student announces the topic in the title and the opening paragraph, and proceeds to map out a clear set of sub-headings that consistently return to the twin ideas of the butterfly-effect structure and generational trauma.  The stated purpose is reiterated in the introduction and conclusion, showing purposeful focus throughout.  Minor lapses in concision (repetition in the conclusion, some digressive plot summary) keep it from feeling fully âexcellent,â yet overall clarity and sense of direction align well with the 5â6 descriptor.

A logical line of argument is evident (Asian-American struggles â visual metaphors â character relationships â nihilism â multiverse), and some evaluation of symbolism and cultural tension appears.  However, counter-arguments, critical self-reflection, and sustained evaluation of secondary voices are limited, so the discussion is âgoodâ rather than âexcellent,â matching the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The essay moves beyond description by connecting cinematic techniques (colour palettes, costume, lighting) to thematic arguments about trauma and the butterfly effect.  Still, many paragraphs rely on generalised commentary and long plot summaries before arriving at analytical points, and evidence is sometimes asserted rather than fully demonstrated.  Support is present but not consistently rigorous, placing the work in the middle of the 4-6 band.

The essay follows IB EE conventions: title page data, table of contents, numbered sections, images with captions, conclusion, and full Works Cited.  Internal signposting ensures readability, and required elements appear complete, satisfying the descriptor for the 3-4 band.

The student displays solid knowledge of the film, its plot, characters and production choices, and references cultural concepts such as filial piety, saving face, and symbolism of colour.  Secondary material is generally understood, though sometimes paraphrased rather than critically integrated, and several claims rest more on assertion than scholarly support.  This level of coherence and largely effective source use aligns with the 3-4 bandâs upper limit.

Film-studies and psychological terms (multiverse, mise-en-scÃ¨ne, nihilism, generational trauma, butterfly effect) are used mostly correctly and consistently.  Occasional imprecision (e.g. equating bipolar disorder with general mood swings) and some informal phrasing prevent the work from reaching the âaccurate and consistentâ standard of the top band, but terminology is more than acceptable for the 3-4 range.

The research question is explicitly worded, sharply delimited to one film, two theoretical lenses (butterfly effect / generational trauma), and a defined cultural context (Chinese-American immigrant families).  Subsequent sections and evidence consistently address that question, demonstrating an effective connection to the discussion.  Some sub-sections (e.g. bipolar disorder) drift slightly beyond the questionâs main scope, hence a solid rather than perfect score.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated, tightly scoped to one film and two theoretical lenses, and connects well to the subsequent analysis.
Including the word count at the start meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to administrative detail.
The title effectively frames the investigationâs focus on the butterfly effect and generational trauma in EEAAO, establishing clear purpose and scope from the outset.
The directorâs name is misspelled as âSchienrt.â Correct to âScheinertâ to ensure accurate citation and credibility.
The Table of Contents is comprehensive and clearly organized, aiding navigation and demonstrating an appropriate structure.
Page numbers are not visible in the footer, making it difficult to confirm alignment with the ToC. Ensure visible numbering on each page.
The opening definition of trauma is broad and relies on general descriptors. Incorporate a peerâreviewed psychological source to strengthen academic rigor.
This paragraph describes the butterfly effect but doesnât tie directly to the filmâs therapeutic resolution of trauma. Link the theory to specific scenes early on.
Section outline in this paragraph provides a useful roadmap and reinforces focus on key themes; signposting supports coherence.
The discussion of Asian American struggles integrates multiple factors effectively, showing good breadth of background knowledge.
The MathML overline in the subsection heading is unnecessary and may confuse readers. Use plain text for clarity.
Figure 1 caption is incomplete; the sentence ends abruptly. Complete the description so readers understand what the image depicts.
This section offers context but drifts from film analysis. Anchor the immigration discussion with direct references to EEAAO scenes.
The analysis asserts that the color wheel represents emotion, but lacks sceneâspecific evidence. Cite a timestamped example showing this effect.
The claim about low selfâesteem and depression requires a full citation (year, page) for Gillespie. Strengthen source traceability.
Linking visual storytelling to the butterfly effect demonstrates solid application of film concepts and cultural theory.
Add the timestamp (e.g., 1:10:05) when discussing Evelynâs red sweater to ground your color analysis in a concrete moment.
The paragraph on universe hues provides interesting thematic insight but wanders before linking back to trauma. Tighten the conclusion.
Figure 2 is wellâcaptioned with scene context and timestamp, reinforcing the analysis visually.
The sentence beginning âNot only do the costumesâ is incomplete. Finish the thought to maintain clarity and flow.
Connecting Joyâs pearlâencrusted outfit to filial duty shows excellent application of cultural context to visual analysis.
This dense paragraph merges multiple ideas. Break into two paragraphs to improve readability and support each claim separately.
Section heading use of bold fonts and numbering helps readers track progression through character analysis.
Figures 5 and 6 are wellâintegrated and support discussion of familial tension, showing good alignment of primary evidence.
The subsection header includes MathML notation (<math>), which disrupts readability. Use plain text titles for consistency.
The treatment of Joyâs bipolar disorder conflates clinical condition with narrative trauma. Distinguish generational trauma from mental health nuances.
Scene analysis of non-linear narrative vividly connects chaos theory to generational trauma, demonstrating strong analytical depth.
Works Cited entries show inconsistent indentation and broken URLs. Apply hanging indent format and verify all links.
Consider including a brief discussion of potential limitations or counter-arguments to strengthen critical evaluation in the conclusion.
The quoted dialogue on nihilism is expertly integrated, showing deep engagement with the filmâs philosophy.
The conclusion repeats the same phrasing in two consecutive paragraphs. Condense to avoid redundancy and sharpen final impact.
